bibliographic_data.title: Identification of SOCS2 and SOCS6 as biomarkers in human colorectal cancer
bibliographic_data.abstract: Over the past years, some members of the family of suppressor of cytokine signalling (SOCS) proteins have emerged as potential tumour suppressors. This study aimed at investigating the clinical significance of SOCS proteins in colorectal carcinoma (CRC). We integrated publicly available microarray expression data on CRC in humans, analysed the expression pattern of SOCSs and assessed the predictive power of SOCS2 and SOCS6 for diagnostic purposes by generating receiver operating characteristic curves. Using laser microdissected patient material we assessed SOCS expression on RNA and protein levels as well as their methylation status in an independent CRC patient cohort. Finally, we investigated the prognostic value of SOCS2 and SOCS6. The meta-analysis as well as the independent patient cohort analysis reveal a stage-independent downregulation of SOCS2 and SOCS6 and identify both molecules as diagnostic biomarkers for CRC. We demonstrate a different methylation pattern within the SOCS2 promoter between tumour tissue and normal control tissue in 25% of CRC patients. Furthermore, early CRC stage patients with low expression of SOCS2 display significantly shorter disease-free survival.

core_content.novelty_claim: The study identifies SOCS2 and SOCS6 as diagnostic biomarkers for colorectal cancer (CRC) through the analysis of their expression patterns, methylation status, and prognostic value in patient cohorts.
core_content.key_methodology: The researchers integrated publicly available microarray data to analyze the expression pattern of SOCS proteins. They used receiver operating characteristic curves to assess the predictive power of SOCS2 and SOCS6 for diagnostic purposes. Additionally, they assessed SOCS expression on RNA and protein levels using laser microdissected patient material in an independent CRC patient cohort.
outcomes: SOCS2 and SOCS6 are downregulated in CRC and serve as diagnostic biomarkers.
A different methylation pattern within the SOCS2 promoter is observed between tumor tissue and normal control tissue in 25% of CRC patients.
Early stage CRC patients with low expression of SOCS2 have significantly shorter disease-free survival.
core_content.full_text_markdown: This study investigates the clinical significance of SOCS proteins, specifically SOCS2 and SOCS6, in colorectal carcinoma (CRC). By analyzing publicly available microarray data, the researchers found a stage-independent downregulation of these proteins. They used receiver operating characteristic curves to assess their diagnostic power and evaluated expression levels on RNA and protein using laser microdissected patient material. The study also examined the methylation status of SOCS2 in tumor tissue compared to normal control tissue. Key findings include the identification of SOCS2 and SOCS6 as biomarkers, a different methylation pattern within the SOCS2 promoter, and shorter disease-free survival for early stage CRC patients with low SOCS2 expression.
